user_query:
I need a clean, board-ready briefing package from yesterday’s legislative briefing. Use only the local files in input/ and produce the following deliverables under output/.

Inputs:
- input/meeting_notes_raw.txt
- input/Legislation_Impact_Memo_Draft.md
- input/finance_assumptions.yaml

Deliverables:
1) output/Meeting_Summary.md
   - Purpose: A concise summary of the meeting that our Board can read before the finance committee meets.
   - Include the following labeled sections in plain text:
     • Bill Overview: bill id and title.
     • Key Provisions: the nurse staffing ratios discussed.
     • Decisions and Follow-ups: decisions taken and action items with owners.
     • Assumptions (from finance_assumptions.yaml): list the numeric and date assumptions used (baseline_annual_nursing_hours, additional_hours_pct, avg_hourly_rn_cost_usd, one_time_training_cost_usd, medicaid_base_rate_bps_change, effective_date, fiscal_year).
     • Projected Financial Impact: compute and state the incremental annual labor cost as (baseline_annual_nursing_hours × additional_hours_pct × avg_hourly_rn_cost_usd), rounded to the nearest dollar, and separately list the one-time training cost. Also note the Medicaid base rate change in basis points.
     • Assumption Check: briefly note any mismatches you found between input/Legislation_Impact_Memo_Draft.md and input/finance_assumptions.yaml (e.g., bill id or outdated figures) and what you corrected.
   - All bill identifiers, dates, and numeric assumptions must match input/finance_assumptions.yaml; use meeting_notes_raw.txt for qualitative points (decisions, risks, follow-ups).

2) output/Legislation_Impact_Memo_Updated.md
   - Take input/Legislation_Impact_Memo_Draft.md and produce an updated, board-facing memo that corrects the bill id/name and replaces any outdated figures with the values from input/finance_assumptions.yaml.
   - The memo should at minimum include: Subject line with the correct bill id, a short Executive Summary (2–4 sentences), a Financial Impact section that includes the computed incremental annual labor cost and the one-time training cost, a Reimbursement Note that mentions the Medicaid base rate change (in bps) and timing, and an Appendix: Assumptions listing the exact YAML keys and values used.
   - Do not remove any sections that are already in the draft; rewrite or fill gaps as needed so all quantitative content aligns with input/finance_assumptions.yaml and the computed cost from (1).

3) output/Board_Email_Draft.txt
   - Draft a plain-text email addressed to the recipients specified in input/finance_assumptions.yaml under recipients.board_to (To:) and recipients.cc (Cc:). Use exactly those addresses.
   - Subject: include the bill id and a short phrase about the financial impact (e.g., “Briefing: SB-842 financial impact summary (FY2025 assumptions)”).
   - Body: 2–3 short paragraphs that summarize the key points from output/Meeting_Summary.md, highlight the projected incremental annual labor cost and one-time training cost, call out the Medicaid base rate change, and note risks and follow-ups. Refer to the two generated files by their paths (output/Meeting_Summary.md and output/Legislation_Impact_Memo_Updated.md).

Constraints and checks:
- Treat input/finance_assumptions.yaml as the source of truth for all numeric assumptions, dates, and the bill id/title. If meeting_notes_raw.txt and the draft memo conflict with the YAML on numbers, use the YAML values; use the notes for decisions/qualitative content.
- The computed incremental annual labor cost in both the summary and updated memo must be the same and must equal: baseline_annual_nursing_hours × additional_hours_pct × avg_hourly_rn_cost_usd (from the YAML), rounded to the nearest dollar.
- Ensure the email To/Cc exactly match the recipients listed in input/finance_assumptions.yaml.
- Do not fetch any external content.

Deliverables should be reproducible from the inputs and must reflect the specific values in the YAML. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
